article_title: Chiplets Enter the Production Era as UCIe 3.0, Massive Packaging Expansions, and Multi-Die AI Accelerators Converge

content_preview.title: Chiplets Enter the Production Era as UCIe 3.0, Massive Packaging Expansions, and Multi-Die AI Accelerators Converge
content_preview.summary: UCIe 3.0 wins its first interoperability demonstrations, TSMC targets 130,000 CoWoS wafers per month by year-end, and Rebellions ships the first quad-chiplet AI accelerator with UCIe interconnects — three signals that the chiplet era is moving from specification to silicon.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

For years, chiplets were the semiconductor industry's perpetual next big thing — a modular approach to chip design that promised to break monolithic silicon's scaling limits but remained confined largely to AMD's CPUs and a handful of FPGA products. In the first quarter of 2026, three developments are converging to suggest that the chiplet era has finally arrived in earnest.

The UCIe 3.0 specification has won its first live cross-vendor interoperability demonstration. TSMC is racin...

source_verification:
- semiengineering.com: CONFIRMED. Chiplet market trajectory, UCIe standardization, yield economics, $600B market by 2031.
- tomshardware.com (Rebellions): CONFIRMED. REBEL-100 quad-chiplet AI accelerator with UCIe, ISSCC 2026, competitive with H200.
- trendforce.com: CONFIRMED. TSMC AP7 Chiayi facility, advanced packaging capex 24% CAGR 2025-2027, CoPoS timeline.
- electronicdesign.com, tomshardware.com (Intel), nature.com: Not independently fetched but claims are consistent with verified sources.
